Task: write a tiny local comparative brief about two approaches to medical AI rollout: autonomy-first vs auditability-first. Return only 2 sentences and 2 short...
summary_text: Autonomy-first is attractive for speed and scale, but in medical AI it should be treated as a narrow exception because the error mode is opaque and responsibility can blur. Auditability-first better matches high-stakes care: slower rollout, clearer traceability, and human judgment at the boundary. - Prefer auditability-first for diagnosis, triage, and treatment support. - Allow autonomy-first only in low-risk, reversible workflows with strong logging and review. consulted_worldview_source: /Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/philosophy.md used_obsidian_for_worldview: no worldview_contract_applied: true
